design_philosophy.core_loop: Guide autonomous lemmings through obstacle courses by assigning limited skill uses to individual creatures, balancing their survival across interconnected levels to accumulate enough survivors for world completion
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited skill uses per level) versus permadeath mechanics (lemmings lost are gone forever within a world) creating cascading consequences where early-level failures force replays
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Orchestrate collective survival through tactical positioning and ability assignment; shepherd a group through increasingly complex environmental hazards
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects variable win conditions in favor of 'deliver one lemming' simplicity; embraces open-ended world order (player chooses which of twelve tribes to tackle); penalty-free level resets via 'nuke' function contradicts the survival-consequence philosophy elsewhere; fast-forward mechanic explicitly designed to allow player pacing control, suggesting opinion that puzzle solving shouldn't be constrained by real-time pressure

difficulty.lives_system: Per-world lemming pool (60 lemmings per world carried across 10 levels); individual lemming permadeath within world progression; pool depletion can force world restarts
difficulty.continues_system: Levels can be individually replayed without penalty to rebuild lemming reserves; 'nuke' function allows reset of current level with no progression loss
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-like structure: world progress saved between worlds; within-world progress requires lemmings to survive sequential levels; gold/silver/bronze medal system tracks optimization but doesn't gate progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player controls skill assignment timing, chooses world order, can fast-forward or pause at will, can replay earlier levels within world to accumulate survivors, can nuke levels for restarts. Minimal difficulty options documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Skill availability per level (pool of specific skills with limited uses); 43 skills total across game; certain skills appear more frequently in specific tribal worlds but no skills exclusive to single tribe. Skills split into categories: mobility enhancement (Runner, Icarus Wings), terrain addition (Builder, Planter), terrain removal (Miner, Bazooker), behavior alteration (Blocker, Attractor)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear within-world progression (completing level N unlocks level N+1 within a world); non-linear between worlds (all twelve tribal worlds available from start, player chooses order). World completion requires gold medal on all 10 levels to secure talisman piece
replayability_hooks: Medal ranking system (gold/silver/bronze based on lemming survival rate) encourages optimization replays
Permadeath carryover mechanics incentivize early-level replays to maximize survivors for harder late-world levels
Practice mode with configurable skill sets (any 8 of 43 skills) on four practice levels
High skill variety (51 total skills across original 8 + 43 new) enables multiple solution approaches per level
Classic tribe variant preserves original Lemmings Blocker skill and behaviors for nostalgia/comparison

world_building_techniques: Tribal theming: twelve culturally-distinct lemming tribes (Highland, Cavelem, Circus, etc.) with visual/cosmetic variation and flavor text
Overarching narrative framing: prophecy of darkness on Lemming Island; talisman collection quest provides meta-goal structure across twelve worlds
Environmental storytelling through tribe-specific level design and obstacle theming
Plot justifies mechanical progression: collect talisman pieces to escape island

faction_indicators: Twelve tribal factions (Highland, Cavelem, Circus, etc.) with distinct cultural identities
Tribal skill frequency distributions suggest playstyle differentiation per faction
danger_signals: Traps and hazards themed per tribal world (implicit from level obstacle descriptions)
Environmental obstacles requiring specific skill solutions (Builder vs Bazooker terrain manipulation reflects resource scarcity conflicts)
environmental_storytelling: Tribal cosmetic variations in lemming appearance and animation
Level design reflecting tribal themes and cultural exuberance
Talisman pieces collected from each tribe suggest political/resource acquisition narrative
